video.title: Giro d'Italia 2020 - Stage 13 Highlights | Cycling | Eurosport

transcript.clean_text: Stage 13 of the Giro d'Italia, the last chance for the fast men perhaps to finish before stage 19 in Asti. The day before the general classification battle was due to resume in the time trial, an opportunity with two short lumps at the end after really flat first three quarters part of the stage. The stage leaving Emilia Romagna heading into the Veneto and finishing in Monselice after the mighty Muro di Calaone, the second of two climbs. Seven riders got in the break. They had a maximum gap of three minutes and no chance on the day really as Bora-Hansgrohe and Israel Start-Up Nation took up the riding early on. Cesare Benedetti went to work alongside Danny Navarro and Maciej Bodnar and it looked as though we'd be in for a day with Peter Sagan trying to put pressure on the thicker leg sprinters. Once they got to the two climbs at the end, the Roccolo famous from the Giro del Veneto, we saw that plan put into action. Arnaud Démare would be dropped but only by a few bike lengths. Over the top of the first climb just two survivors of the breakaway, Bouchard and Tonelli, but only 30 seconds ahead of the maglia rosa group. Démare would indeed get back on the group alongside Elia Viviani with 20 km to go. There'd be one problem with that. We'd have one more really steep climb. Tonelli and Bouchard finally caught a kilometer from the top of that last climb of the day and Sagan alongside Swift distance but only just. We saw a few little moves at the top, Gaviria and Hodeg going. Guerreiro, his former teammate in the under 23s as well, and Almeida, another man from that team, up there in the pink jersey. This Giro all about the Axeon Hagens Berman alumni. On the way in, Sagan would get to within 15 seconds. But the GC group led on by the brilliant Deceuninck Quick-Step domestiques would continue to extend time. Masnada and Knox doing a sterling job. Sagan alongside the Ghent-Wevelgem Swift having to go it alone with Ballerini sitting on. It would be the GC group that would get there. A strange sprint awaited messy stuff, but infiltrating his way amongst the pink jersey favorites would be Diego Ulissi. Ulissi going to the line, Almeida in second place, Konrad in third. Bonus seconds won for the maglia rosa as he extended his lead. And Ulissi picking up stage win number two of this Giro by half a wheel length. That'll be his eighth in total. Good rides too from Sam Bennett and Geoghegan Hart behind. Celebration though for Diego Ulissi. Vincenzo Nibali in that top 10, full signing as well. No big differences at the top in the general classification, but Almeida and Konrad picking up six and four seconds respectively. And João Almeida going into the time trial now leads by 40 seconds over Kelderman and 49 from Bilbao. He will have an 11th day in the maglia rosa, 34.1 km in Prosecco country, three time checks along the way, and the big Muro Ca' del Poggio, the most difficult part of the route. Conegliano to Valdobbiadene, the second and most difficult of the time trials. Another GC day, a day before the next mountain stage. Mhm.
